ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging
- (optionally) run_dbt() — execute the dbt project and see the result

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- You MUST call run_dbt() after writing or editing models and before declaring the project complete.
- If run_dbt() reports errors, read the failing model files, fix the SQL, and call run_dbt() again until it succeeds or you cannot fix the errors.
- Do NOT skip run_dbt() to save time — the orchestrator will run dbt again and fail if SQL is invalid.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 0, 51, 48, 214522, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_master_v2_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_master_v2_src"."master_assets" LIMIT 10`.


## fixture_master_v2_src.master_assets  (800 rows)

Columns:
- `asset_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `asset_name` text NULL
- `serien_nummer` text NULL
- `garantieende` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `projekt_kennung` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_kontakte  (403 rows)

Columns:
- `kontakt_id` text NOT NULL
- `rufname` text NULL
- `familienname` text NULL
- `kontakt_email` text NULL
- `tel` text NULL
- `berufsbezeichnung` text NULL
- `rolle` text NULL
- `korrespondenzsprache` text NULL
- `kd_nummer` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_kunden  (210 rows)

Columns:
- `kundennummer` text NOT NULL
- `unternehmensname` text NULL
- `erp_nr` text NULL
- `kundenklasse` text NULL
- `vertriebsgebiet` text NULL
- `industrie` text NULL
- `homepage` text NULL
- `stadt` text NULL
- `land_region` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_opportunities  (540 rows)

Columns:
- `opp_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `titel` text NULL
- `vertriebsphase` text NULL
- `zieldatum` text NULL
- `auftragswert` text NULL
- `waehrungscode` text NULL
- `kunden_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_projekte  (317 rows)

Columns:
- `projekt_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `projektname` text NULL
- `projektstatus` text NULL
- `go_live_datum` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `opp_kennung_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

# Target schema


## Account
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `ERP_Number__c` text
- `Customer_Tier__c` text in (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum)
- `Region__c` text
- `Industry` text
- `Website` text
- `BillingCity` text
- `BillingCountry` text
- `Legacy_Customer_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Contact
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `FirstName` text
- `LastName` text NOT NULL
- `Email` text
- `Phone` text
- `Title` text
- `Role__c` text in (Decision Maker, End User, Technical Contact, Executive Sponsor)
- `Preferred_Language__c` text in (DE, EN, FR, ES, IT)
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Contact_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Opportunity
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `StageName` text NOT NULL in (Prospecting, Qualification, Needs Analysis, Value Proposition, Id. Decision Makers, Perception Analysis, Proposal/Price Quote, Negotiation/Review, Closed Won, Closed Lost)
- `CloseDate` text NOT NULL
- `Amount` double precision
- `CurrencyIsoCode` text
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Project__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Project_Status__c` text in (Active, Completed, In Planning, On Hold, Cancelled)
- `Go_Live_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Opportunity__c` text
- `Legacy_Project_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Installed_Asset__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Serial_Number__c` text
- `Warranty_End_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Project__c` text
- `Legacy_Asset_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

You MUST produce exactly one dbt model per target table (5 total): Account, Contact, Opportunity, Project__c, Installed_Asset__c.

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.


Produce the dbt project that transforms the source into the target. Write one model file per target table listed above — all tables are required.

Self-correction is ON: after writing models, call run_dbt(), fix any errors, and read back every model file you edit before finishing.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 0, 51, 48, 214530, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 0, 51, 48, 214900, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f3f35-a575-7660-a190-1454ba5834c6', conversation_id='019f3f35-a575-7660-a190-1453a37744a4')

land_region\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_kunden\') }}\\n)\\n\\nSELECT\\n  -- Generate deterministic Salesforce-style Id\\n  SUBSTRING(MD5(\'Account_\' || kundennummer) FROM 1 \\"Region__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Industry\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(industrie), \'\') AS \\"Industry\\",\\n  \\n  -- Website\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(homepage), \'\') AS \\"Website\\",\\n  \\n  -- Billing City\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(stadt), \'\') AS \\"BillingCity\\",\\n  \\n  -- Billing Country\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(land_region), \'\') AS \\"BillingCountry\\",\\n  \\n  -- Legacy Customer ID\\n  kundennummer AS ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content='Now let me create the Opportunity model:'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args='{"path": "models/Opportunity.sql", "content": "{{ config(materialized=\'table\') }}\\n\\nWITH opportunity_data AS (\\n  SELECT\\n    opp_kennung,\\n    titel,\\n    vertriebsphase,\\n    zieldatum,\\n    auftragswert,\\n    waehrungscode,\\n    kunden_ref\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_opportunities\') }}\\n),\\n\\naccount_mapping AS (\\n  SELECT\\n    kundennummer AS legacy_customer_id,\\n    SUBSTRING(MD5(\'Account_\' || kundennummer) FROM 1 FOR 18) AS account_id\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_kunden\') }}\\n),\\n\\n-- Parse dates from various formats\\nparsed_dates AS (\\n  SELECT\\n    opp_kennung,\\n    titel,\\n    vertriebsphase,\\n    waehrungscode,\\n    kunden_ref,\\n    -- Parse auftragswert: handle currency prefixes and various formats\\n    CASE \\n      WHEN auftragswert IS NULL OR TRIM(auftragswert) IN (\'\', \'None\', \'null\') THEN NULL\\n      WHEN auftragswert ~ \'^[0-9\\\\-]+\\\\.?[0-9]*$\' THEN auftragswert::DOUBLE PRECISION\\n      WHEN auftragswert ~ \'^[0-9\\\\-]+,[0-9]+$\' THEN \\n        -- European format: 1.234,56 -> 1234.56\\n        REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert, \'[.]\', \'\', \'g\')::DOUBLE PRECISION / \\n        POWER(10, LENGTH(SPLIT_PART(auftragswert, \',\', 2)))\\n      ELSE \\n        -- Try to extract numeric value from strings like \\"EUR 144893.69\\"\\n        CASE \\n          WHEN auftragswert ~ \'([0-9]+[.,]?[0-9]*)\' THEN \\n            REGEXP_REPLACE(\\n              REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert, \'[^0-9.,\\\\-]\', \'\', \'g\'),\\n              \'^([0-9]+)\\\\.([0-9]+),([0-9]+)$\', \'\\\\1\\\\2.\\\\3\',\\n              \'g\'\\n            )::DOUBLE PRECISION\\n          ELSE NULL\\n        END\\n    END AS amount_value,\\n    \\n    -- Parse zieldatum from various formats\\n    CASE \\n      WHEN zieldatum IS NULL OR TRIM(zieldatum) IN (\'\', \'N/A\', \'0000-00-00\') THEN NULL\\n      WHEN zieldatum ~ \'^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}$\' THEN \\n        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(zieldatum, \'YYYY-MM-DD\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      WHEN zieldatum ~ \'^[0-9]{2}\\\\.[0-9]{2}\\\\.[0-9]{4}$\' THEN \\n        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(zieldatum, \'DD.MM.YYYY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      WHEN zieldatum ~ \'^[0-9]{2}/[0-9]{2}/[0-9]{4}$\' THEN \\n        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(zieldatum, \'MM/DD/YYYY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      WHEN zieldatum ~ \'^[0-9]{8}$\' THEN \\n        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(zieldatum, \'YYYYMMDD\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      WHEN zieldatum ~ \'^[0-9]{4}[0-9]{2}[0-9]{2}$\' THEN \\n        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(zieldatum, \'YYYYMMDD\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      ELSE NULL\\n    END AS close_date\\n  FROM opportunity_data\\n)\\n\\nSELECT\\n  -- Generate deterministic Salesforce-style Id\\n  SUBSTRING(MD5(\'Opportunity_\' || p.opp_kennung) FROM 1 FOR 18) AS \\"Id\\",\\n  \\n  -- Name: use titel\\n  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(p.titel), \'\'), p.opp_kennung) AS \\"Name\\",\\n  \\n  -- StageName: normalize to enum values\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'PROSPECTING\', \'PROSPECT\', \'IN KONTAKT\') THEN \'Prospecting\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'QUALIFICATION\', \'QUALI\', \'QUALIFIKATION\', \'IN PRÜFUNG\') THEN \'Qualification\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'NEEDS ANALYSIS\') THEN \'Needs Analysis\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'VALUE PROPOSITION\') THEN \'Value Proposition\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'ID. DECISION MAKERS\', \'ENTSCHEIDER\') THEN \'Id. Decision Makers\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'PERCEPTION ANALYSIS\') THEN \'Perception Analysis\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'PROPOSAL/PRICE QUOTE\', \'PROPOSAL\') THEN \'Proposal/Price Quote\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'NEGOTIATION/REVIEW\', \'NEGOTIATION\') THEN \'Negotiation/Review\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'CLOSED WON\', \'CLOSED WON\', \'ABGESCHLOSSEN (GEWONNEN)\', \'GEWONNEN\', \'WON\') THEN \'Closed Won\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'CLOSED LOST\', \'CLOSED LOST\', \'ABGESCHLOSSEN (VERLOREN)\', \'VERLOREN\', \'LOST\') THEN \'Closed Lost\'\\n    ELSE \'Prospecting\'\\n  END AS \\"StageName\\",\\n  \\n  -- CloseDate: required, use parsed date or current date as fallback\\n  COALESCE(p.close_date, TO_CHAR(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, \'YYYY-MM-DD\')) AS \\"CloseDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- Amount\\n  p.amount_value AS \\"Amount\\",\\n  \\n  -- CurrencyIsoCode\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.waehrungscode)) IN (\'EUR\', \'EURO\', \'€\') THEN \'EUR\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.waehrungscode)) IN (\'USD\', \'DOLLAR\', \'$\') THEN \'USD\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.waehrungscode)) IN (\'CHF\', \'SWISS FRANC\') THEN \'CHF\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.waehrungscode)) IN (\'GBP\', \'POUND\') THEN \'GBP\'\\n    ELSE UPPER(TRIM(p.waehrungscode))\\n  END AS \\"CurrencyIsoCode\\",\\n  \\n  -- AccountId: lookup from master_kunden via kunden_ref (KD-MXXXX -> CUST-MXXXX)\\n  am.account_id AS \\"AccountId\\",\\n  \\n  -- Legacy Opportunity ID\\n  p.opp_kennung AS \\"Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- CreatedDate\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- LastModifiedDate\\n config(materialized=\'table\') }}\\n\\nWITH project_data AS (\\n  SELECT\\n    projekt_kennung,\\n    projektname,\\n    projektstatus,\\n    go_live_datum,\\n    kunden_kennung,\\n    opp_kennung_ref\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_projekte\') }}\\n),\\n\\naccount_mapping AS (\\n  SELECT\\n    kundennummer AS legacy_customer_id,\\n    SUBSTRING(MD5(\'Account_\' || kundennummer) FROM 1 FOR 18) AS account_id\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_kunden\') }}\\n),\\n\\nopportunity_mapping AS (\\n  SELECT\\n    opp_kennung AS legacy_opportunity_id,\\n    SUBSTRING(MD5(\'Opportunity_\' || opp_kennung) FROM 1 FOR 18) AS opportunity_id\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_opportunities\') }}\\n),\\n\\n-- Parse dates from various formats\\nparsed_dates AS (\\n  SELECT\\n    projekt_kennung,\\n    projektname,\\n    projektstatus,\\n    kunden_kennung,\\n    opp_kennung_ref,\\n    -- Parse go_live_datum from various formats\\n    CASE \\n      WHEN go_live_datum IS NULL OR TRIM(go_live_datum) IN (\'\', \'N/A\', \'0000-00-00\') THEN NULL\\n      WHEN go_live_datum ~ \'^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}$\' THEN \\n        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(go_live_datum, \'YYYY-MM-DD\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      WHEN go_live_datum ~ \'^[0-9]{2}\\\\.[0-9]{2}\\\\.[0-9]{4}$\' THEN \\n        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(go_live_datum, \'DD.MM.YYYY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      WHEN go_live_datum ~ \'^[0-9]{2}/[0-9]{2}/[0-9]{4}$\' THEN \\n        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(go_live_datum, \'MM/DD/YYYY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      WHEN go_live_datum ~ \'^[0-9]{8}$\' THEN \\n        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(go_live_datum, \'YYYYMMDD\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      WHEN go_live_datum ~ \'^[0-9]{4}[0-9]{2}[0-9]{2}$\' THEN \\n        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(go_live_datum, \'YYYYMMDD\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      ELSE NULL\\n    END AS go_live_date\\n  FROM project_data\\n)\\n\\nSELECT\\n  -- Generate deterministic Salesforce-style Id\\n  SUBSTRING(MD5(\'Project_\' || p.projekt_kennung) FROM 1 FOR 18) AS \\"Id\\",\\n  \\n  -- Name: use projektname\\n  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(p.projektname), \'\'), p.projekt_kennung) AS \\"Name\\",\\n  \\n  -- Project Status: normalize to enum values\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.projektstatus)) IN (\'ACTIVE\', \'AKTIV\') THEN \'Active\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.projektstatus)) IN (\'COMPLETED\', \'ABGESCHLOSSEN\', \'COMPLETED\') THEN \'Completed\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.projektstatus)) IN (\'IN PLANNING\', \'IN PLANUNG\', \'PLANUNG\') THEN \'In Planning\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.projektstatus)) IN (\'ON HOLD\', \'PAUSIERT\') THEN \'On Hold\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.projektstatus)) IN (\'CANCELLED\', \'STORNIERT\', \'CANCELLED\') THEN \'Cancelled\'\\n    ELSE \'In Planning\'\\n  END AS \\"Project_Status__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Go Live Date\\n  p.go_live_date AS \\"Go_Live_Date__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Account__c: lookup from master_kunden via kunden_kennung\\n  am.account_id AS \\"Account__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Opportunity__c: lookup from master_opportunities via opp_kennung_ref\\n  om.opportunity_id AS \\"Opportunity__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Legacy Project ID\\n  p.projekt_kennung AS \\"Legacy_Project_ID__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- CreatedDate\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- LastModifiedDate\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- IsDeleted: default to 0\\n  0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\n\\nFROM parsed_dates p\\nLEFT JOIN account_mapping am ON p.kunden_kennung = am.legacy_customer_id\\nLEFT JOIN opportunity_mapping om ON REPLACE(p.opp_kennung_ref, \'OPP-M-\', \'OPP-\') = om.legacy_opportunity_id\\n"}', tool_call_id='RtrMQpsXS')], THEN \\n        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(garantieende, \'YYYYMMDD\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      WHEN garantieende ~ \'^[0-9]{4}[0-9]{2}[0-9]{2}$\' THEN \\n        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(garantieende, \'YYYYMMDD\'), FROM 1 FOR 18) AS \\"Id\\",\\n  \\n  -- Name: use asset_name\\n  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.asset_name), \'\'), a.asset_kennung) AS \\"Name\\",\\n  \\n  -- Serial Number\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(a.serien_nummer), am.account_id AS \\"Account__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Project__c: lookup from master_projekte via projekt_kennung\\n  pm.project_id AS \\"Project__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Legacy Asset ID\\n  a.asset_kennung AS \\"Legacy_Asset_ID__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- CreatedDate\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- LastModifiedDate\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging
- (optionally) run_dbt() — execute the dbt project and see the result

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- You MUST call run_dbt() after writing or editing models and before declaring the project complete.
- If run_dbt() reports errors, read the failing model files, fix the SQL, and call run_dbt() again until it succeeds or you cannot fix the errors.
- Do NOT skip run_dbt() to save time — the orchestrator will run dbt again and fail if SQL is invalid.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 0, 51, 48, 214522, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_master_v2_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_master_v2_src"."master_assets" LIMIT 10`.


## fixture_master_v2_src.master_assets  (800 rows)

Columns:
- `asset_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `asset_name` text NULL
- `serien_nummer` text NULL
- `garantieende` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `projekt_kennung` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_kontakte  (403 rows)

Columns:
- `kontakt_id` text NOT NULL
- `rufname` text NULL
- `familienname` text NULL
- `kontakt_email` text NULL
- `tel` text NULL
- `berufsbezeichnung` text NULL
- `rolle` text NULL
- `korrespondenzsprache` text NULL
- `kd_nummer` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_kunden  (210 rows)

Columns:
- `kundennummer` text NOT NULL
- `unternehmensname` text NULL
- `erp_nr` text NULL
- `kundenklasse` text NULL
- `vertriebsgebiet` text NULL
- `industrie` text NULL
- `homepage` text NULL
- `stadt` text NULL
- `land_region` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_opportunities  (540 rows)

Columns:
- `opp_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `titel` text NULL
- `vertriebsphase` text NULL
- `zieldatum` text NULL
- `auftragswert` text NULL
- `waehrungscode` text NULL
- `kunden_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_projekte  (317 rows)

Columns:
- `projekt_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `projektname` text NULL
- `projektstatus` text NULL
- `go_live_datum` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `opp_kennung_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

# Target schema


## Account
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `ERP_Number__c` text
- `Customer_Tier__c` text in (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum)
- `Region__c` text
- `Industry` text
- `Website` text
- `BillingCity` text
- `BillingCountry` text
- `Legacy_Customer_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Contact
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `FirstName` text
- `LastName` text NOT NULL
- `Email` text
- `Phone` text
- `Title` text
- `Role__c` text in (Decision Maker, End User, Technical Contact, Executive Sponsor)
- `Preferred_Language__c` text in (DE, EN, FR, ES, IT)
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Contact_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Opportunity
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `StageName` text NOT NULL in (Prospecting, Qualification, Needs Analysis, Value Proposition, Id. Decision Makers, Perception Analysis, Proposal/Price Quote, Negotiation/Review, Closed Won, Closed Lost)
- `CloseDate` text NOT NULL
- `Amount` double precision
- `CurrencyIsoCode` text
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Project__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Project_Status__c` text in (Active, Completed, In Planning, On Hold, Cancelled)
- `Go_Live_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Opportunity__c` text
- `Legacy_Project_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Installed_Asset__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Serial_Number__c` text
- `Warranty_End_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Project__c` text
- `Legacy_Asset_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

You MUST produce exactly one dbt model per target table (5 total): Account, Contact, Opportunity, Project__c, Installed_Asset__c.

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.


Produce the dbt project that transforms the source into the target. Write one model file per target table listed above — all tables are required.

Self-correction is ON: after writing models, call run_dbt(), fix any errors, and read back every model file you edit before finishing.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 0, 51, 48, 214530, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 0, 51, 48, 214900, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f3f35-a575-7660-a190-1454ba5834c6', conversation_id='019f3f35-a575-7660-a190-1453a37744a4')

land_region\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_kunden\') }}\\n)\\n\\nSELECT\\n  -- Generate deterministic Salesforce-style Id\\n  SUBSTRING(MD5(\'Account_\' || kundennummer) FROM 1 FOR 18) AS \\"Id\\",\\n  \\n  -- Name: use unternehmensname if available, otherwise kundennummer\\n  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(unternehmensname), \'\'), kundennummer) AS \\"Name\\",\\n  \\n  -- ERP Number\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(erp_nr), \'\') AS \\"ERP_Number__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Customer Tier: normalize to enum values\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN (\'PLATINUM\', \'PLATIN\') THEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN (\'BRONZE\', \'BRONZE\') THEN \'Bronze\'\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"Customer_Tier__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Region\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(vertriebsgebiet), \'\') AS \\"Region__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Industry\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(industrie), \'\') AS \\"Industry\\",\\n  \\n  -- Website\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(homepage), \'\') AS \\"Website\\",\\n  \\n  -- Billing City\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(stadt), \'\') AS \\"BillingCity\\",\\n  \\n  -- Billing Country\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(land_region), \'\') AS \\"BillingCountry\\",\\n  \\n  -- Legacy Customer ID\\n  kundennummer AS \\"Legacy_Customer_ID__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- CreatedDate: use current timestamp for now\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- LastModifiedDate: use current timestamp for now\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- IsDeleted: default to 0\\n  0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\n\\nFROM customer_data\\n"}', c.kontakt_id) FROM 1 FOR 18) AS \\"Id\\",\\n  \\n  -- FirstName\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(c.rufname), \'\') AS \\"FirstName\\",\\n  \\n  -- LastName: required, use familienname or kontakt_id as fallback\\n  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(c.familienname), \'\'), c.kontakt_id) AS \\"LastName\\",\\n  \\n  -- Email\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(c.kontakt_email), \'\') AS \\"Email\\",\\n  \\n  -- Phone\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(c.tel), \'\') AS \\"Phone\\",\\n  \\n  -- Title\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(c.berufsbezeichnung), \'\') AS \\"Title\\",\\n  \\n  -- Role: normalize to enum values\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.rolle)) IN (\'DECISION formats\\n    CASE \\n      WHEN auftragswert IS NULL OR TRIM(auftragswert) IN (\'\', \'None\', \'null\') THEN NULL\\n      WHEN auftragswert ~ \'^[0-9\\\\-]+\\\\.?[0-9]*$\' THEN auftragswert::DOUBLE PRECISION\\n      WHEN auftragswert ~ \'^[0-9\\\\-]+,[0-9]+$\' THEN \\n        -- European format: 1.234,56 -> 1234.56\\n        REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert, \'[.]\', \'\', \'g\')::DOUBLE PRECISION / \\n        POWER(10, LENGTH(SPLIT_PART(auftragswert, \',\', 2)))\\n      ELSE \\n        -- Try to extract numeric value from strings like \\"EUR 144893.69\\"\\n        CASE \\n          WHEN auftragswert ~ \'([0-9]+[.,]?[0-9]*)\' THEN \\n            REGEXP_REPLACE(\\n              REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert, \'[^0-9.,\\\\-]\', \'\', \'g\'),\\n              \'^([0-9]+)\\\\.([0-9]+),([0-9]+)$\', \'\\\\1\\\\2.\\\\3\',\\n              \'g\'\\n            )::DOUBLE PRECISION\\n          ELSE NULL\\n        END\\n    END AS amount_value,\\n    \\n    -- Parse zieldatum from various formats\\n    CASE \\n      WHEN zieldatum IS NULL OR TRIM(zieldatum) IN (\'\', \'N/A\', \'0000-00-00\') THEN NULL\\n      WHEN zieldatum ~ \'^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}$\' THEN \\n        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(zieldatum, \'YYYY-MM-DD\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      WHEN zieldatum ~ \'^[0-9]{2}\\\\.[0-9]{2}\\\\.[0-9]{4}$\' THEN \\n        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(zieldatum, \'DD.MM.YYYY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      WHEN zieldatum ~ \'^[0-9]{2}/[0-9]{2}/[0-9]{4}$\' THEN \\n        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(zieldatum, \'MM/DD/YYYY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      WHEN zieldatum ~ \'^[0-9]{8}$\' THEN \\n        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(zieldatum, \'YYYYMMDD\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      WHEN zieldatum ~ \'^[0-9]{4}[0-9]{2}[0-9]{2}$\' THEN \\n        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(zieldatum, \'YYYYMMDD\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      ELSE NULL\\n    END AS close_date\\n  FROM opportunity_data\\n)\\n\\nSELECT\\n  -- Generate deterministic Salesforce-style Id\\n  SUBSTRING(MD5(\'Opportunity_\' || p.opp_kennung) FROM 1 FOR 18) AS \'PROPOSAL\') THEN \'Proposal/Price Quote\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'NEGOTIATION/REVIEW\', \'NEGOTIATION\') THEN \'Negotiation/Review\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'CLOSED WON\', \'CLOSED WON\', \'ABGESCHLOSSEN (GEWONNEN)\', \'GEWONNEN\', \'WON\') THEN \'Closed Won\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'CLOSED LOST\', \'CLOSED LOST\', \'ABGESCHLOSSEN (VERLOREN)\', \'VERLOREN\', \'LOST\') THEN \'Closed Lost\'\\n    ELSE \'Prospecting\'\\n  END AS \\"StageName\\",\\n  \\n  -- CloseDate: required, use parsed date or current date as fallback\\n  COALESCE(p.close_date, TO_CHAR(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, \'YYYY-MM-DD\')) AS \\"CloseDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- Amount\\n  p.amount_value AS \\"Amount\\",\\n  \\n  -- CurrencyIsoCode\\n  CASE \\n messages.raw:
ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging
- (optionally) run_dbt() — execute the dbt project and see the result

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

2. **Account.sql** - Transform master_kunden
3. **Contact.sql** - Transform master_kontakte with Account lookup

ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content='Now let me create the Installed_Asset__c model:'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args='{"path": "models/Installed_Asset__c.sql", "content": "{{ config(materialized=\'table\') }}\\n\\nWITH asset_data AS (\\n  SELECT\\n    asset_kennung,\\n    asset_name,\\n    serien_nummer,\\n    garantieende,\\n    kunden_kennung,\\n    projekt_kennung\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_assets\') }}\\n),\\n\\naccount_mapping AS (\\n  SELECT\\n    kundennummer AS legacy_customer_id,\\n    SUBSTRING(MD5(\'Account_\' || kundennummer) FROM 1 FOR 18) AS account_id\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_kunden\') }}\\n),\\n\\nproject_mapping AS (\\n  SELECT\\n    projekt_kennung AS legacy_project_id,\\n    SUBSTRING(MD5(\'Project_\' || projekt_kennung) FROM 1 FOR 18) AS project_id\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_projekte\') }}\\n),\\n\\n-- Parse dates from various formats\\nparsed_dates AS (\\n  SELECT\\n    asset_kennung,\\n    asset_name,\\n    serien_nummer,\\n    kunden_kennung,\\n    projekt_kennung,\\n    -- Parse garantieende from various formats\\n    CASE \\n      WHEN garantieende IS NULL OR TRIM(garantieende) IN (\'\', \'N/A\', \'0000-00-00\') THEN NULL\\n      WHEN garantieende ~ \'^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}$\' THEN \\n        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(garantieende, \'YYYY-MM-DD\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      WHEN garantieende ~ \'^[0-9]{2}\\\\.[0-9]{2}\\\\.[0-9]{4}$\' THEN \\n        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(garantieende, \'DD.MM.YYYY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      WHEN garantieende ~ \'^[0-9]{2}/[0-9]{2}/[0-9]{4}$\' THEN \\n        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(garantieende, \'MM/DD/YYYY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      WHEN garantieende ~ \'^[0-9]{8}$\' THEN \\n        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(garantieende, \'YYYYMMDD\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      WHEN garantieende ~ \'^[0-9]{4}[0-9]{2}[0-9]{2}$\' THEN \\n        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(garantieende, \'YYYYMMDD\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      ELSE NULL\\n    END AS warranty_end_date\\n  FROM asset_data\\n)\\n\\nSELECT\\n  -- Generate deterministic Salesforce-style Id\\n  SUBSTRING(MD5(\'Asset_\' || a.asset_kennung) FROM 1 FOR 18) AS \\"Id\\",\\n  \\n  -- Name: use asset_name\\n  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.asset_name), \'\'), a.asset_kennung) AS \\"Name\\",\\n  \\n  -- Serial Number\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(a.serien_nummer), \'\') AS \\"Serial_Number__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Warranty End Date\\n  a.warranty_end_date AS \\"Warranty_End_Date__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Account__c: lookup from master_kunden via kunden_kennung\\n  am.account_id AS \\"Account__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Project__c: lookup from master_projekte via projekt_kennung\\n  pm.project_id AS \\"Project__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Legacy Asset ID\\n  a.asset_kennung AS \\"Legacy_Asset_ID__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- CreatedDate\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- LastModifiedDate\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- IsDeleted: default to 0\\n  0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\n\\nFROM parsed_dates a\\nLEFT JOIN account_mapping am ON a.kunden_kennung = am.legacy_customer_id\\nLEFT JOIN project_mapping pm ON REPLACE(a.projekt_kennung, \'PROJ-M-\', \'PROJ-\') = pm.legacy_project_id\\n"}', tool_call_id='htLEX5P8N')], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=17563, output_tokens=999), ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging
- (optionally) run_dbt() — execute the dbt project and see the result

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- You MUST call run_dbt() after writing or editing models and before declaring the project complete.
- If run_dbt() reports errors, read the failing model files, fix the SQL, and call run_dbt() again until it succeeds or you cannot fix the errors.
- Do NOT skip run_dbt() to save time — the orchestrator will run dbt again and fail if SQL is invalid.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 0, 51, 48, 214522, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_master_v2_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_master_v2_src"."master_assets" LIMIT 10`.


## fixture_master_v2_src.master_assets  (800 rows)

Columns:
- `asset_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `asset_name` text NULL
- `serien_nummer` text NULL
- `garantieende` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `projekt_kennung` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_kontakte  (403 rows)

Columns:
- `kontakt_id` text NOT NULL
- `rufname` text NULL
- `familienname` text NULL
- `kontakt_email` text NULL
- `tel` text NULL
- `berufsbezeichnung` text NULL
- `rolle` text NULL
- `korrespondenzsprache` text NULL
- `kd_nummer` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_kunden  (210 rows)

Columns:
- `kundennummer` text NOT NULL
- `unternehmensname` text NULL
- `erp_nr` text NULL
- `kundenklasse` text NULL
- `vertriebsgebiet` text NULL
- `industrie` text NULL
- `homepage` text NULL
- `stadt` text NULL
- `land_region` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_opportunities  (540 rows)

Columns:
- `opp_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `titel` text NULL
- `vertriebsphase` text NULL
- `zieldatum` text NULL
- `auftragswert` text NULL
- `waehrungscode` text NULL
- `kunden_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_projekte  (317 rows)

Columns:
- `projekt_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `projektname` text NULL
- `projektstatus` text NULL
- `go_live_datum` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `opp_kennung_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

# Target schema


## Account
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `ERP_Number__c` text
- `Customer_Tier__c` text in (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum)
- `Region__c` text
- `Industry` text
- `Website` text
- `BillingCity` text
- `BillingCountry` text
- `Legacy_Customer_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Contact
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `FirstName` text
- `LastName` text NOT NULL
- `Email` text
- `Phone` text
- `Title` text
- `Role__c` text in (Decision Maker, End User, Technical Contact, Executive Sponsor)
- `Preferred_Language__c` text in (DE, EN, FR, ES, IT)
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Contact_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Opportunity
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `StageName` text NOT NULL in (Prospecting, Qualification, Needs Analysis, Value Proposition, Id. Decision Makers, Perception Analysis, Proposal/Price Quote, Negotiation/Review, Closed Won, Closed Lost)
- `CloseDate` text NOT NULL
- `Amount` double precision
- `CurrencyIsoCode` text
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Project__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Project_Status__c` text in (Active, Completed, In Planning, On Hold, Cancelled)
- `Go_Live_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Opportunity__c` text
- `Legacy_Project_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Installed_Asset__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Serial_Number__c` text
- `Warranty_End_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Project__c` text
- `Legacy_Asset_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

You MUST produce exactly one dbt model per target table (5 total): Account, Contact, Opportunity, Project__c, Installed_Asset__c.

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.


Produce the dbt project that transforms the source into the target. Write one model file per target table listed above — all tables are required.

Self-correction is ON: after writing models, call run_dbt(), fix any errors, and read back every model file you edit before finishing.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 0, 51, 48, 214530, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 0, 51, 48, 214900, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f3f35-a575-7660-a190-1454ba5834c6', conversation_id='019f3f35-a575-7660-a190-1453a37744a4')

}}\\n\\nWITH customer_data AS (\\n  SELECT\\n    kundennummer,\\n    unternehmensname,\\n    erp_nr,\\n    kundenklasse,\\n    vertriebsgebiet,\\n    industrie,\\n    homepage,\\n    stadt,\\n    land_region\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_kunden\') }}\\n)\\n\\nSELECT\\n  -- Generate deterministic Salesforce-style Id\\n  SUBSTRING(MD5(\'Account_\' || kundennummer) FROM 1 FOR 18) AS \\"Id\\",\\n  \\n  -- Name: use unternehmensname if available, otherwise kundennummer\\n  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(unternehmensname), \'\'), kundennummer) AS \\"Name\\",\\n  \\n  -- ERP Number\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(erp_nr), \'\') AS \\"ERP_Number__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Customer Tier: normalize to enum values\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN (\'PLATINUM\', \'PLATIN\') THEN \'Platinum\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN (\'GOLD\', \'GOLDEN\') THEN \'Gold\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN (\'SILVER\', \'SILBER\') THEN \'Silver\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN (\'BRONZE\', \'BRONZE\') THEN \'Bronze\'\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"Customer_Tier__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Region\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(vertriebsgebiet), \'\') AS \\"Region__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Industry\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(industrie), \'\') AS \\"Industry\\",\\n  \\n  -- Website\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(homepage), \'\') AS \\"Website\\",\\n  \\n  -- Billing City\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(stadt), \'\') AS \\"BillingCity\\",\\n  \\n  -- Billing Country\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(land_region), \'\') AS \\"BillingCountry\\",\\n  \\n  -- Legacy Customer ID\\n  kundennummer AS \\"Legacy_Customer_ID__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- CreatedDate: use current timestamp for now\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- LastModifiedDate: use current timestamp for now\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- IsDeleted: default to 0\\n  0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\n\\nFROM customer_data\\n"}', FOR 18) AS account_id\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_kunden\') }}\\n)\\n\\nSELECT\\n  -- Generate deterministic Salesforce-style Id\\n  SUBSTRING(MD5(\'Contact_\' || c.kontakt_id) FROM 1 FOR 18) AS \\"Id\\",\\n  \\n  -- FirstName\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(c.rufname), \'\') AS \\"FirstName\\",\\n  \\n  -- LastName: required, use familienname or kontakt_id as fallback\\n  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(c.familienname), \'\'), c.kontakt_id) AS \\"LastName\\",\\n  \\n  -- Email\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(c.kontakt_email), \'\') AS \\"Email\\",\\n  \\n  -- Phone\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(c.tel), \'\') AS \\"Phone\\",\\n  \\n  -- Title\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(c.berufsbezeichnung), \'\') AS \\"Title\\",\\n  \\n  -- Role: normalize to enum values\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.rolle)) IN (\'DECISION MAKER\', \'ENTSCHEIDER\') THEN \'Decision Maker\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.rolle)) IN (\'END USER\', \'ENDANWENDER\', \'END USER\') THEN \'End User\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.rolle)) IN (\'TECHNICAL CONTACT\', \'TECHNISCHER ANSPRECHPARTNER\', \'TECHNIKER\') THEN \'Technical Contact\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.rolle)) IN (\'EXECUTIVE SPONSOR\', \'SPONSOR\') THEN \'Executive Sponsor\'\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"Role__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Preferred Language: normalize to 2-letter codes\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.korrespondenzsprache)) IN (\'DEUTSCH\', \'DE\', \'GERMAN\') THEN \'DE\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.korrespondenzsprache)) IN (\'ENGLISH\', \'EN\', \'ENGLISCH\') THEN \'EN\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.korrespondenzsprache)) IN (\'FRANZÖSISCH\', \'FR\', \'FRENCH\') THEN \'FR\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.korrespondenzsprache)) IN (\'ESPAÑOL\', \'ES\', \'SPANISH\') THEN \'ES\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.korrespondenzsprache)) IN (\'ITALIANO\', \'IT\', \'ITALIENISCH\') THEN \'IT\'\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"Preferred_Language__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- AccountId: lookup from master_kunden via kd_nummer\\n  am.account_id AS \\"AccountId\\",\\n  \\n  -- Legacy Contact ID\\n  c.kontakt_id AS \\"Legacy_Contact_ID__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- CreatedDate\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- LastModifiedDate\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- IsDeleted: default to 0\\n  0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\n\\nFROM contact_data c\\nLEFT JOIN account_mapping am ON c.kd_nummer = am.legacy_customer_id\\n"}', ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content='Now let me create the Opportunity model:'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args='{"path": "models/Opportunity.sql", "content": "{{ config(materialized=\'table\') }}\\n\\nWITH opportunity_data AS (\\n  SELECT\\n    opp_kennung,\\n    titel,\\n    vertriebsphase,\\n    zieldatum,\\n    auftragswert,\\n    waehrungscode,\\n    kunden_ref\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_opportunities\') }}\\n),\\n\\naccount_mapping AS (\\n  SELECT\\n    kundennummer AS legacy_customer_id,\\n    SUBSTRING(MD5(\'Account_\' || kundennummer) FROM 1 FOR 18) AS account_id\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_kunden\') }}\\n),\\n\\n-- Parse dates from various formats\\nparsed_dates AS (\\n  SELECT\\n    opp_kennung,\\n    titel,\\n    vertriebsphase,\\n    waehrungscode,\\n    kunden_ref,\\n    -- Parse auftragswert: handle currency prefixes and various formats\\n    CASE \\n      WHEN auftragswert IS NULL OR TRIM(auftragswert) IN (\'\', \'None\', \'null\') THEN NULL\\n      WHEN auftragswert ~ \'^[0-9\\\\-]+\\\\.?[0-9]*$\' THEN auftragswert::DOUBLE PRECISION\\n      WHEN auftragswert ~ \'^[0-9\\\\-]+,[0-9]+$\' THEN \\n        -- European format: 1.234,56 -> 1234.56\\n        REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert, \'[.]\', \'\', \'g\')::DOUBLE PRECISION / \\n        POWER(10, LENGTH(SPLIT_PART(auftragswert, \',\', 2)))\\n      ELSE \\n        -- Try to extract numeric value from strings like \\"EUR 144893.69\\"\\n        CASE \\n          WHEN auftragswert ~ \'([0-9]+[.,]?[0-9]*)\' THEN \\n            REGEXP_REPLACE(\\n              REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert, \'[^0-9.,\\\\-]\', \'\', \'g\'),\\n              \'^([0-9]+)\\\\.([0-9]+),([0-9]+)$\', \'\\\\1\\\\2.\\\\3\',\\n              \'g\'\\n            )::DOUBLE PRECISION\\n          ELSE NULL\\n        END\\n    END AS amount_value,\\n    \\n    -- Parse zieldatum from various formats\\n    CASE \\n      WHEN zieldatum IS NULL OR TRIM(zieldatum) IN (\'\', \'N/A\', \'0000-00-00\') THEN NULL\\n      WHEN zieldatum ~ \'^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}$\' THEN \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      WHEN zieldatum ~ \'^[0-9]{2}/[0-9]{2}/[0-9]{4}$\' THEN \\n        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(zieldatum, \'MM/DD/YYYY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      WHEN zieldatum ~ \'^[0-9]{8}$\' THEN \\n ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging
- (optionally) run_dbt() — execute the dbt project and see the result

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- You MUST call run_dbt() after writing or editing models and before declaring the project complete.
- If run_dbt() reports errors, read the failing model files, fix the SQL, and call run_dbt() again until it succeeds or you cannot fix the errors.
- Do NOT skip run_dbt() to save time — the orchestrator will run dbt again and fail if SQL is invalid.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 0, 51, 48, 214522, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_master_v2_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_master_v2_src"."master_assets" LIMIT 10`.


## fixture_master_v2_src.master_assets  (800 rows)

Columns:
- `asset_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `asset_name` text NULL
- `serien_nummer` text NULL
- `garantieende` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `projekt_kennung` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_kontakte  (403 rows)

Columns:

- `rufname` text NULL
- `familienname` text NULL

- `tel` text NULL
- `berufsbezeichnung` text NULL
- `rolle` text NULL
- `korrespondenzsprache` text NULL
- `kd_nummer` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_kunden  (210 rows)

Columns:
- `kundennummer` text NOT NULL
- `unternehmensname` text NULL
- `erp_nr` text NULL
- `kundenklasse` text NULL
- `vertriebsgebiet` text NULL
- `industrie` text NULL

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_opportunities  (540 rows)

Columns:

- `titel` text NULL
- `vertriebsphase` text NULL
- `zieldatum` text NULL
- `auftragswert` text NULL
- `waehrungscode` text NULL
- `kunden_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_projekte  (317 rows)

Columns:

- `projektname` text NULL
- `projektstatus` text NULL
- `go_live_datum` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `opp_kennung_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

# Target schema


## Account
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `ERP_Number__c` text
- `Customer_Tier__c` text in (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum)
- `Region__c` text
- `Industry` text
- `Website` text
- `BillingCity` text
- `BillingCountry` text
- `Legacy_Customer_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Contact
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `FirstName` text
- `LastName` text NOT NULL
- `Email` text
- `Phone` text
- `Title` text
- `Role__c` text in (Decision Maker, End User, Technical Contact, Executive Sponsor)
- `Preferred_Language__c` text in (DE, EN, FR, ES, IT)
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Contact_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Opportunity
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `StageName` text NOT NULL in (Prospecting, Qualification, Needs Analysis, Value Proposition, Id. Decision Makers, Perception Analysis, Proposal/Price Quote, Negotiation/Review, Closed Won, Closed Lost)
- `CloseDate` text NOT NULL
- `Amount` double precision
- `CurrencyIsoCode` text
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Project__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Project_Status__c` text in (Active, Completed, In Planning, On Hold, Cancelled)
- `Go_Live_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Opportunity__c` text
- `Legacy_Project_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Installed_Asset__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Serial_Number__c` text
- `Warranty_End_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Project__c` text
- `Legacy_Asset_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

You MUST produce exactly one dbt model per target table (5 total): Account, Contact, Opportunity, Project__c, Installed_Asset__c.

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.


Produce the dbt project that transforms the source into the target. Write one model file per target table listed above — all tables are required.

Self-correction is ON: after writing models, call run_dbt(), fix any errors, and read back every model file you edit before finishing.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 0, 51, 48, 214530, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 0, 51, 48, 214900, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f3f35-a575-7660-a190-1454ba5834c6', conversation_id='019f3f35-a575-7660-a190-1453a37744a4')

formats\\n    CASE \\n      WHEN auftragswert IS NULL OR TRIM(auftragswert) IN (\'\', \'None\', \'null\') THEN NULL\\n      WHEN auftragswert ~ \'^[0-9\\\\-]+\\\\.?[0-9]*$\' THEN auftragswert::DOUBLE PRECISION\\n      WHEN auftragswert ~ \'^[0-9\\\\-]+,[0-9]+$\' THEN \\n        -- European format: 1.234,56 -> 1234.56\\n        REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert, \'[.]\', \'\', \'g\')::DOUBLE PRECISION / auftragswert ~ \'([0-9]+[.,]?[0-9]*)\' THEN \\n            REGEXP_REPLACE(\\n              REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert, \'[^0-9.,\\\\-]\', \'\', \'g\'),\\n              \'^([0-9]+)\\\\.([0-9]+),([0-9]+)$\', \'\\\\1\\\\2.\\\\3\',\\n              \'g\'\\n            )::DOUBLE PRECISION\\n          ELSE NULL\\n        END\\n    END AS amount_value,\\n    \\n    -- Parse zieldatum from various formats\\n    CASE \\n      WHEN zieldatum IS NULL OR TRIM(zieldatum) IN (\'\', \'N/A\', \'0000-00-00\') THEN NULL\\n      WHEN zieldatum ~ \'^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}$\' THEN      TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(zieldatum, \'YYYYMMDD\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      WHEN zieldatum ~ \'^[0-9]{4}[0-9]{2}[0-9]{2}$\' THEN \\n        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(zieldatum, \'YYYYMMDD\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      ELSE NULL\\n    END AS close_date\\n  FROM opportunity_data\\n)\\n\\nSELECT\\n  -- Generate deterministic Salesforce-style Id\\n  SUBSTRING(MD5(\'Opportunity_\' || p.opp_kennung) FROM 1 FOR 18) AS \'ABGESCHLOSSEN (VERLOREN)\', \'VERLOREN\', \'LOST\') THEN \'Closed Lost\'\\n    ELSE \'Prospecting\'\\n  END AS \\"StageName\\",\\n  \\n  -- CloseDate: required, use parsed date or current date as p.projekt_kennung) AS \\"Name\\",\\n  \\n  -- Project Status: normalize to enum values\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.projektstatus)) IN (\'ACTIVE\', \'AKTIV\') THEN \'Active\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.projektstatus)) IN (\'COMPLETED\', \'ABGESCHLOSSEN\', \'COMPLETED\') THEN \'Completed\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.projektstatus)) IN (\'IN PLANNING\', \'IN PLANUNG\', \'PLANUNG\') THEN \'In Planning\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.projektstatus)) IN (\'ON HOLD\', \'PAUSIERT\') THEN \'On Hold\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p.projektstatus)) IN (\'CANCELLED\', \'STORNIERT\', \'CANCELLED\') THEN \'Cancelled\'\\n    ELSE \'In Planning\'\\n  END AS \\"Project_Status__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Go Live Date\\n  p.go_live_date AS \\"Go_Live_Date__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Account__c: lookup from master_kunden via Project ID\\n  p.projekt_kennung AS \\"Legacy_Project_ID__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- CreatedDate\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- LastModifiedDate\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- IsDeleted: default to 0\\n  0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\n\\nFROM parsed_dates p\\nLEFT JOIN account_mapping am ON p.kunden_kennung = am.legacy_customer_id\\nLEFT JOIN opportunity_mapping om ON REPLACE(p.opp_kennung_ref, \'OPP-M-\', \'OPP-\') = om.legacy_opportunity_id\\n"}', tool_call_id='RtrMQpsXS')], formats\\nparsed_dates AS (\\n  SELECT\\n    asset_kennung,\\n    asset_name,\\n    serien_nummer,\\n    kunden_kennung,\\n    projekt_kennung,\\n    -- Parse garantieende from various formats\\n THEN \\n        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(garantieende, \'YYYYMMDD\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      WHEN garantieende ~ \'^[0-9]{4}[0-9]{2}[0-9]{2}$\' THEN \\n        TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(garantieende, \'YYYYMMDD\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n      ELSE NULL\\n    END AS warranty_end_date\\n  FROM asset_data\\n)\\n\\nSELECT\\n  -- Generate deterministic Salesforce-style Id\\n  SUBSTRING(MD5(\'Asset_\' || a.asset_kennung) am.account_id AS \\"Account__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Project__c: lookup from master_projekte via projekt_kennung\\n  pm.project_id AS \\"Project__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Legacy Asset ID\\n  a.asset_kennung AS \\"Legacy_Asset_ID__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- CreatedDate\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- LastModifiedDate\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- IsDeleted: default to 0\\n  0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\n\\nFROM parsed_dates a\\nLEFT JOIN account_mapping am ON a.kunden_kennung = am.legacy_customer_id\\nLEFT JOIN
